content: ICM curiosity-driven exploration by autonomous agents can be reoriented to actively probe the working memory capacity boundaries of LLMs, turning intrinsic reward for exploring novel state transitions corresponding to untested reasoning step sequences to systematically map how working memory limits that current studies only passively observe. This creates an autonomous curiosity-driven search can generate datasets of failure cases that directly reveal how working memory constraints across different reasoning tasks, rather than relying on manual test design.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
